text: A myriad of complex natural products and biologically active molecules contain core aromatic ring systems that require precise modifications, namely individual atomic substitutions, to generate desired analogues for evaluation. Consequently, skeletal editing has proven to be a reliable and versatile method for such structural modifications. Although tremendous advances in the arene skeletal editing technology using either carbene or nitrene insertions accompanied by subsequent complex rearrangements have been demonstrated during the last decade, these approaches appear not to be applicable to pyridines. Here, we have demonstrated skeletal editing of pyridines using a formally CN-to-CC atom-pair exchange procedure by preparing, in situ, the reactive heterocyclic species by initial dearomatization of pyridines and the subsequent [4+2] cycloaddition between these reactive species and functionalized azulenes, which is then simply followed by rearomatization through a retro-cycloaddition to provide the desired hetero-and non-heterocyclic compounds with fully controlled substitution in a single-step (one-pot) reaction protocol. Notably, cycloaddition with modified azulene congeners allows for the unique introduction of substituents of choice in the final hetero on the condensed aromatic ring system. Late-stage skeletal editing has been successfully demonstrated using this technology for several existing FDA drugs that contain a pyridine ring as a critical element of activity.
